clinical.chief_complaints: Intermittent mild epigastric discomfort for 5 days, no vomiting, no fever, no weight loss
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Mrs. Kalaivani Murugesan, a 38-year-old female homemaker, presents with intermittent mild epigastric/upper abdominal discomfort for the past 5 days, described as a dull, non-radiating ache. No association with food intake. No nausea, vomiting, fever, jaundice, or weight loss. No alteration in bowel or bladder habits. Symptoms have been gradually reducing over the last 2 days with over-the-counter antacids. No prior similar episodes. Patient is ambulatory and otherwise well. Presented to OPD for evaluation; admitted for observation and work-up at family's request.
clinical.provisional_diagnosis: Non-specific dyspepsia, functional in nature — ICD-10: K30, rule out gastritis
clinical.planned_procedure: Conservative management — observation, symptomatic treatment, no surgical or interventional procedure planned at this stage

examination_findings.general: Conscious, cooperative, well-nourished, no pallor, icterus, cyanosis, clubbing, or lymphadenopathy
examination_findings.rs: Air entry bilateral, clear, no added sounds
examination_findings.abdomen: Soft, mild epigastric tenderness on deep palpation, no guarding or rigidity, no organomegaly, no palpable mass, bowel sounds normal

investigations.result_summary: CBC: Hb 12.6 g/dL, WBC 6800/cumm, Platelets 2.6 lakh. LFT: Bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL, SGOT 24 U/L, SGPT 28 U/L. RFT: Creatinine 0.7 mg/dL. Blood Sugar Fasting: 88 mg/dL. Amylase/Lipase within normal limits.
USG Abdomen (19/06/2026): Liver, gallbladder, pancreas, kidneys, spleen — normal study. No gallstones, no free fluid, no mass lesion. Normal abdominal ultrasound.
Normal sinus rhythm. No ST-T changes.

clinical.past_medical_history: No significant medical history. No chronic illnesses.
current_medications: Antacid syrup OTC, self-medicated for 2 days prior to admission
clinical.notes: Mild, self-limiting, non-specific dyspepsia. All investigations normal — no gallstones, no organomegaly, no abnormal LFT/amylase/lipase. No red-flag symptoms (no weight loss, no GI bleed, no jaundice, no persistent vomiting). Symptoms already improving with conservative measures. No clinical indication for any surgical or interventional PM-JAY package. Negative test case — Stage 1/2 should find no validated packages and route to BLOCKED or USP_RECOMMENDED. No PDFs to be generated for this TC regardless of pipeline outcome.
